task: Check if a visa is required to work in the UK for longer than 6 months in Healthcare as an American citizen.
graderReasoning: The agent used the official GOV.UK visa checker, selected nationality 'United States of America', purpose 'Work, academic visit or business', duration 'longer than 6 months', and job type 'Health and care professional'. The resulting GOV.UK page explicitly stated: 'You need a visa to work in health and care.' The final answer accurately reflects the website evidence and includes relevant visa routes shown on the result page.
final_result_response:
Yes. GOV.UK says an American citizen needs a visa to work in UK health and care for longer than 6 months. <citation refs="fetch_call_VkBeU4Z1ZtNBLsfaqSAJoBIE|fc_0643fe38f1979cdd016a04fffa72188199bd788797b11c9d67#1">“You need a visa to work in health and care” based on answers: United States of America, work, longer than 6 months, health and care professional.</citation>

Likely routes shown include the Health and Care Worker visa or Skilled Worker visa. For the Health and Care Worker visa, GOV.UK says you need a UK job offer from a registered sponsor, a minimum salary, and usually proof of English.
action_history: Opening GOV.UK: const p1 = await openTab('https://www.gov.uk/');
Searching GOV.UK visa checker: await page.locator('e5').fill('check if you need a UK visa work healthcare longer than 6 months');
Searching GOV.UK visa checker: await page.locator('e6').click();
Finding visa checker result: await page.locator('e8').fill('Check if you need a UK visa');
Finding visa checker result: await page.locator('e9').click();
Selecting longer than 6 months: await page.locator('e9').click();
